job_title: Data Scientist II, SCOT - OSS Buying Outcomes
strengths: Hands-on ETL/ELT and data pipeline builds
weaknesses: No explicit deep learning/production ML deployment
Relatively short formal data scientist tenure
Limited mentoring or senior-level leadership evidence
improvement_bullets: Designed and executed A/B tests and observational studies, applying causal inference methodologies to isolate campaign lift and measure the incremental impact of marketing interventions.
Led model backtesting frameworks and performance validation experiments to ensure statistical rigor before deploying new buying algorithms into production environments.
Mentored junior team members on SQL optimization and Python best practices, driving a culture of technical excellence and shared accountability for project outcomes.
Collaborated with engineering teams to integrate large-scale data workflows, ensuring model reliability and performance consistency within production-grade machine learning pipelines.
Leveraged advanced statistical modeling to analyze feature interactions, providing actionable insights into cost and buying scenarios while mentoring peers on interpreting model triggers.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Data Scientist II, SCOT - OSS Buying Outcomes opportunity with Amazon. I am interested in this opportunity because of Amazon’s unparalleled ability to solve complex, global-scale logistical challenges through data-driven innovation. What interests me about working for Amazon is its approach to obsessing over the customer experience through technical precision, which intrigues me because of my own background in automating systems to drive tangible business efficiency. I believe my expertise in building scalable ELT/ETL pipelines and my proficiency in statistical modeling would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a focus on business-centric outcomes. In fact, at Shine Masters, I identified a gap in data-driven marketing and created a statistical model to identify high-value customer segments; this project informed targeted marketing initiatives that contributed to a 300% increase in revenue for that segment over 12 months. Ultimately, I know that the Data Scientist II, SCOT - OSS Buying Outcomes role at Amazon would allow me to bring my analytical rigor to the core of your retail inventory systems. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst & Operations Lead at Shine Masters enabled me to develop my technical leadership skills. I took initiative in standardizing organizational KPIs and data governance frameworks to create a single source of truth for all stakeholders, which reduced report discrepancies and improved cross-functional alignment. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in causal inference and experimentation to assist in optimizing global inventory management at Amazon. I know that my experience in automated data pipelines and statistical analysis aligns with what it takes to succeed at the Data Scientist II, SCOT - OSS Buying Outcomes role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
